raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Reports completion of a confessional step in a structured recovery process",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Wrongs exist and have an 'exact nature' that can be identified",
    "Admission to God, self, and another person is feasible and meaningful",
    "Such admission produces benefit or progress"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent as a procedural claim; coherence depends on acceptance of a theistic framework and the value of external confession",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The individual accepts the framework of a 12-step program, believes in a higher power, and finds value in explicit moral inventory",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Presented as a universal moral requirement outside recovery contexts or used to imply that non-admission is always harmful or incomplete"
}
